name: BlazBlue: Continuum Shift Extend
description: The second entry in the critically acclaimed BlazBlue franchise, BlazBlue: Continuum Shift Extend adds a number of gameplay modes, story scenarios, new moves, and an all-new playable character to further enhance the BlazBlue experience and solidify its status as one of the all-time great 2D fighters
requirements:
Minimum:
  • OS *: Windows 7 / 8 / 8.1
  • Processor: Intel Core2 Duo
  • Memory: 2 GB RAM
  • Graphics: nVidia GeForce 7900 GT or better / AMD Radeon X1900 / nVidia GeForce GT 620 (Windows 8.1)
  • DirectX: Version 9.0c
  • Network: Broadband Internet connection
  • Storage: 10 GB available space
  • Sound Card: Direct Sound
comment1:
The ultimate mix of a visual novel and a easy to learn, hard to master fighting game. I came for the interesting character designs and graphics and stayed for the great dialogue.

If you want to follow the story it's still possible to skip the first one (Calamity Trigger) because this game includes a playable recap of the events in that game, which was mostly just a huge amount of conflicting timelines anyway.
comment2: Best combat for a beginner fighting game, Not punishing like +R, also nice story mode and single player modes. I recommend this title to anyone who wants to get into fighting games.
comment3: Much smoother combos than Calamity trigger thanks to stylish fighting technique
